title:

Read each passage and choose the best word or phrase from among the four choices for each blank. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and mark your answer.
description:
Friendship with Mrs. Philip 
 
 Ellie is a high school student. She and her parents moved to a town by the sea last year. When Ellie's family moved in, their neighbor, Mrs. Philip, brought them a cherry pie to welcome them to the neighborhood. It was delicious. Ellie asked Mrs. Philip to teach her how to make it. One day, Mrs. Philip happily ( 1 ). During that time, they enjoyed cooking and talking together.
 
 Ellie often visited Mrs. Philip's house and did her homework after school on weekdays. Mrs. Philip sometimes helped Ellie with it. However, last month, Mrs. Philip had to leave home for a month to take care of her sick brother, who lived far away. Ellie ( 2 ) every day. So, she started exchanging e-mails with Mrs. Philip. When Mrs. Philip finally came back, Ellie brought her a cherry pie to welcome her back.

choices: told Ellie about a good bakery
felt very sad and missed her

title:

description:
Jack's cookies

For Mother's Day, Jack decided to bake some cookies for his mother. Jack's mother always does everything for him. He had never even ( 1 ), but he tried his best to make delicious cookies without any help. He did not forget to use milk, flour, and of course, white powder which was stored in a plastic box with a large "S" on it.

On the next day, Jack gave the cookies to his mother to show gratitude. She got really happy and ate them in front of Jack. After drinking water, Jack's mother smiled and said, "I am proud of your effort to make these cookies by yourself! But you used a wrong ingredient that starts with an 'S'. Next time, you might as well use sugar ( 2 )!"

choices: been in to the living room

title:
【Grade Pre-2 No.3】
Read the following passage and answer the questions below.
description:
Why Danish feel happy

The World Happiness Report is a survey that shows global happiness degree, conducted by United Nations*. Results of this kind of surveys can change depending on targeted countries and intentions, especially given that happiness is a very personal feeling, which ( 1 ). In this report by United Nations, they measure happiness with a questionnaire answered by citizens of each country. According to the World Happiness Report 2018, Denmark was ranked as one of the three happiest countries for 6 straight years among 156 countries.

Why Danish people evaluate themselves and their lives so positive? Denmark is famous for its high taxes. However, most of the Danish ( 2 ) because of the world's top class education and free medical services. Danish believe that the social system improves with higher taxes. But this is not the only reason why Danish tend to feel happier than others.

There is a special word "hygge" in the Danish language. It is impossible to perfectly translate the word into English or any other language. The key to understanding this word is a close trust relationship with people around. It has a close meaning to "a feeling of comfort and satisfaction ( 3 )". They put great value on this delightful time with family and friends they love, which leads them to feel happy.

Happiness is one of the most important feelings for humans because it gives us the power to live. That's why from ancient times, lots of thought and laws were created to protect and sustain people's happiness.

questions.question: Choose the appropriate word to fit in ( 1 ).
choices: only a limitted type of people have
is exactly the same with everyone
was found only a couple of years ago

questions.question: Choose the appropriate word to fit in ( 2 ).
choices: are reluctant to pay such a high tax
do not know they are happy
are happy to pay such high taxes
questions.answer: are happy to pay such high taxes

questions.question: Choose the appropriate word to fit in ( 3 ).
choices: obeying a law to pay lots of tax
